tasks.title: Build Actor resolver + context-layer authorization
tasks.description: Resolve Clerk user to typed Actor (Customer | StaffUser); enforce permissions inside contexts, not only middleware.
tasks.details: Pure resolveActorFromSession(ClerkSessionShape) maps Clerk session → Actor without hitting Clerk's runtime; resolveCurrentActor() is the thin Next-runtime wrapper. requireActor / requireCan / requireStaff guards in src/shared/infra/auth/require.ts throw AuthenticationError (→ 401) and AuthorizationError (→ 403) sharing an abstract AuthError base. requireStaff supports a minimum-role constraint. Integration test demonstrates the guarded-route-handler pattern with 401/403/200 mapping. Local customer/staff_user lookup lands in task #11 (resolver currently surfaces clerkUserId as actor.id).
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for `can()`; integration test rejects forbidden actions from a route handler.
tasks.completedNotes: src/shared/infra/auth/resolver.ts exposes resolveActorFromSession (pure, fully testable) and resolveCurrentActor (calls Clerk's auth() + currentUser() in route-handler context). Resolver maps Clerk session → Actor: anonymous when no userId; staff when sessionClaims.org_role matches isStaffRole; customer otherwise. Defensive: unrecognized org_role values fall back to customer; missing email throws (cannot construct CustomerActor). require.ts: AuthError abstract base + AuthenticationError (401) + AuthorizationError (403, carries the denied Action). requireActor narrows away AnonymousActor; requireCan combines auth + can() check with anon-permitted actions short-circuiting before the auth check; requireStaff narrows to StaffActor and supports minimum-role constraint via hasAtLeastRole. tests/integration/auth/route-guards.test.ts demonstrates the canonical guarded-route-handler pattern with 401/403/200 outcomes for anonymous/customer/analyst/validator/admin against qa.signoff (validator-gated per .claude/rules/human-qa-required.md). 23 new tests RED-then-GREEN; 116/116 pass; lint + typecheck clean. Bug caught + fixed: AuthError class needed `abstract` keyword for the abstract status property. Local customers.id / staff_users.id lookup ships in task #11; the resolver currently surfaces clerkUserId as actor.id.

tasks.completedNotes: Customer recovery workspace UI (3-pane) at /app/case/[id]/recovery per PRD 02. Server component resolves the recovery path by loading the case (getCaseRepo), the screener session (getScreenerRepo.findSessionById), running determineRecoveryPath, and calling recoveryPlanFor + renderOutreachKit. Three panes: Left — RecoveryStatusPanel shows case id, status banner with path label + SLA, document checklist (one row per plan.acceptedDocs), prerequisite checks (required vs optional), uploaded list when docs exist. Center — OutreachKitPanel renders rendered subject + body verbatim + a CopyButton (client component) that writes "Subject: X\n\n<body>" to navigator.clipboard with "Copied" feedback; attachments-needed list; template version footer. Right — UploadPanel wraps the existing UploadZone with the case-id binding. Zero UI conditionals on path per ADR 015 — everything reads from the plan. 404 paths: case not found; case has no resolvable recovery path (no screener session OR disqualified). not-found.tsx renders an editorial fallback. Auth: middleware gates /app; fine customer-to-case ownership scoping is a follow-up (#52). 16 new tests (6 status-panel + 5 outreach-panel + 5 integration-page via stubbed repos). 592/592 pass; lint + typecheck clean; build green (22 routes — /app/case/[id]/recovery at 2.97kB + 114kB first-load, dynamic-rendered).

tasks.details: Drag-region copy from PDF (P1 polish — basic copy/paste in MVP).
tasks.testStrategy: Integration: save entry → row exists with sourceRecordId + audit row.
tasks.completedNotes: Manual entry-extraction form + provenance write per PRD 02 + PRD 07. Schema (also satisfies #55): entries (id ent_*, caseId FK→cases RESTRICT NOT NULL, entryNumber, entryDate, importerOfRecord, dutyAmountUsdCents bigint, htsCodes text[], phaseFlag, validatedAt/validatedBy, createdAt/updatedAt + UNIQUE(case_id, entry_number) for canonical dedupe + index on (case,time)) and entry_source_records (id esrc_*, entryId FK→entries RESTRICT NOT NULL, recoverySourceId FK→recovery_sources RESTRICT NOT NULL — provenance never null per .ralph/PROMPT.md hard rule, rawData jsonb, confidence enum default pending, extractedAt/extractedBy + indexes on entry/source). drizzle/0006_entries_provenance.sql generated; migration name normalized in _journal.json. EntriesRepo (in-memory + Drizzle) with saveExtractedEntry transactional contract (entry + source insert in one db.transaction). Service saveExtractedEntry composes entry write + audit log row (kind=entry.extracted on first save, entry.source_attached on second-source-attached path) — single source of truth for analyst-facing persistence. POST /api/cases/[id]/entries route: Zod-validated body (entryNumber, dates, htsCodes ≤20, dutyAmountUsdCents non-negative, recoverySourceId required), 400 on bad body, 404 on missing case, 200 with outcome + entry + sourceRecord + auditId on success. ExtractionFormPanel wired to POST when no onSave seam — requires recoverySourceId prop (caller passes from active doc); shows error state if missing. 12 new tests (6 service + 6 integration route); 637/637 pass; lint + typecheck clean; build green (24 routes — added /api/cases/[id]/entries).

tasks.title: USER-TEST: Full recovery workflow
tasks.description: Simulate a paid Recovery Service case from purchase through entry list ready.
tasks.details: Customer purchases; uploads docs; analyst extracts; case transitions to entry_list_ready; customer notified.
tasks.testStrategy: End-to-end Playwright + manual review.
tasks.completedNotes: USER-TEST checkpoint #8 (full recovery workflow). Implementation-side scaffolding for the four legs of the workflow is complete: (1) Stripe Checkout (#36) — POST /api/checkout opens a Session per (sku, tier); /api/webhooks/stripe dedupes via processed_stripe_events UNIQUE + publishes platform/payment.completed. (2) Customer recovery workspace (#51) — /app/case/[id]/recovery: 3-pane with status banner, outreach kit, upload zone; renders correctly given a case + screener-session pair. (3) Ops workspace + extraction form (#52, #53) — /ops/case/[id]: case header + extraction form + side-by-side document viewer; POST /api/cases/[id]/entries persists entry + entry_source_record + audit_log row inside one db.transaction; idempotent on (case_id, entry_number). (4) Lifecycle email cadences (#28-#31) — workflows registered for screener nudges + entry-list-ready + concierge upsell + reengagement. GAPS for the human walk (carry from #38): (a) the webhook does not yet write a Payment row OR auto-create a Case from platform/payment.completed — that orchestration workflow is the missing seam; without it the human walk needs to manually createCase with a screenerSessionId before /app/case/[id]/recovery resolves. (b) The extraction form requires a recoverySourceId, which currently must be supplied as a static prop — wiring DocumentViewerPanel's active doc → ExtractionFormPanel.recoverySourceId is a follow-up; for v1 the wiring requires a recovery_source row to exist for each uploaded document, which is also a missing seam. 637/637 pass. Human owes: provision Stripe test mode + R2 + Inngest dev server + Postgres; manually create a case + recovery_source for a customer; walk the customer through the workspace → upload → staff opens ops workspace → extracts an entry → confirm audit trail in the database. The end-to-end orchestration workflow that closes both gaps lands as part of the v1-launch wave (likely #80+).

tasks.title: Entry-number canonicalization function
tasks.description: src/contexts/entries/canonicalize.ts — strips whitespace, normalizes separators, validates CBP format.
tasks.details: Pure function, fully unit-tested with edge cases.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests cover known good/bad/edge formats.
tasks.completedNotes: Entry-number canonicalization function per PRD 07. canonicalizeEntryNumber(raw) accepts dashed/undashed forms, leading/trailing whitespace, en/em dashes (customers copy-paste from Excel/Word), spaces between segments, mixed case; returns canonical FFF-SSSSSSS-C uppercase or a typed rejection ({reason: empty | length | filer_code_invalid | sequence_not_digits | check_digit_not_digit}). CANONICAL_ENTRY_NUMBER_RE freezes the canonical shape; formatCanonicalEntryNumber throws loudly on non-canonical input so callers can't accidentally render raw entry numbers. Check-digit math deferred to Phase 2 (v1 validates shape only). When the caller supplied dashes (3 raw segments) a malformed filer surfaces as filer_code_invalid rather than generic length — more useful for the analyst review queue. Entries context public surface at src/contexts/entries/index.ts. 24 new tests covering 7 happy formats + 11 rejection paths + raw preservation + canonical regex shape check + display-form throw-on-non-canonical. 661/661 pass; lint + typecheck clean; build green (24 routes — pure module, no client-bundle impact).

tasks.title: Dedupe + fuzzy match
tasks.description: Exact-match dedupe on canonicalized number; fuzzy match on (date + IOR) within tolerance flagged for analyst.
tasks.details: Surface 'review pair' UI in ops workspace.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit + integration tests against fixture sets.
tasks.completedNotes: Dedupe + fuzzy match per PRD 07. classifyEntries(incoming, existing) returns one ClassifiedEntry per incoming row with one of five outcomes: exact_duplicate (canonical match against existing — caller should attach a second source instead of inserting), duplicate_in_batch (same canonical number repeats inside the incoming list after the first occurrence), fuzzy_review_pair (matches existing on date+IOR but DIFFERENT canonical number — both records kept; analyst review queue surfaces the pair per PRD 07 acceptance), new (no match, insert), invalid (canonicalization failed — surface in manual-correction queue with the canonicalResult attached). Pure function — composes with the entries repo in #58+. Fuzzy key is (date + IOR-lowercase-trimmed); requires both halves; v1 uses exact-date match (date-window tolerance deferred to Phase 2). Exact match always wins over fuzzy match. 12 new tests covering exact match (mixed case + dashes), duplicate_in_batch ordering, fuzzy match (case-insensitive IOR, date-mismatch → new, ior-mismatch → new, missing field → new), invalid (gibberish), empty input, exact-wins-over-fuzzy precedence. 673/673 pass; lint + typecheck clean; build green (24 routes — pure module, no client-bundle impact).

tasks.title: Window + phase tagging
tasks.description: Tag every entry with IEEPA window status and phase flag; window version pinned per batch.
tasks.details: Window dates centralized in a single config.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests cover boundary dates.
tasks.completedNotes: Window + phase tagging per PRD 07. CURRENT_IEEPA_WINDOW (version="ieepa-v1-2024", start 2024-04-01, end 2025-12-31, four phases tiling the window contiguously: phase_1_2024_h2, phase_2_2024_q4, phase_3_2025_h1, phase_4_2025_h2 — with TODO(human-action) for legal/customs sign-off on the exact boundaries). IEEPA_WINDOWS array carries every registered window (one for now; future versions append). Pure tagEntry({entryDate}, window) → {inWindow, windowVersion, phaseFlag} — windowVersion is ALWAYS the supplied window's version so batch-level pinning works (a batch tagged today against ieepa-v1-2024 is reproducible after the window is updated). Phase boundary semantics are start-inclusive (a date sitting on a phase boundary lands in the LATER phase). Out-of-window dates (before start or after end) → inWindow=false, phaseFlag=null. Malformed or missing dates → same (inWindow=false, phaseFlag=null) but windowVersion still pinned for audit. 12 new tests covering config invariants (contiguous tiling, unique phase ids), happy paths (in-window, last day), boundary dates (day before / day after / phase boundary start-inclusive), missing/invalid input, and version-pinning. 685/685 pass; lint + typecheck clean; build green (24 routes — pure module).

tasks.title: ACE CSV parser
tasks.description: Parse user-uploaded ACE export CSVs into entry candidates with high source-confidence.
tasks.details: Use papaparse + Zod schema. Rejects malformed rows with structured error.
tasks.testStrategy: Golden-file tests against anonymized ACE fixture CSVs.
tasks.completedNotes: ACE CSV parser per PRD 07. parseAceCsv(csv) → AceParseResult discriminated union: {ok:true, rows: AceEntryCandidate[], errors: AceParseError[]} on header-validated CSV; {ok:false, reason:"missing_columns", missingColumns: AceColumnKey[]} on missing required column. Permissive header normalization (trim + lowercase + underscore→space; synonyms accept "Entry No"/"Entry Number"/"Entry_No", "Total Duty"/"Total Duty (USD)", etc.) but strict per-row validation: entry-number canonicalized via canonicalizeEntryNumber (any failure → field=entryNumber); date must be YYYY-MM-DD ISO; IOR must be non-empty; duty parsed from $-prefixed/comma-separated dollar string to non-negative cents; HTS codes split on ;/comma/whitespace and validated against 4.2.4 digit-dot pattern. Source confidence stamped "high" per PRD 07 source hierarchy (ACE export is the cleanest signal). Per-row failures collected as AceParseError {row, field, reason} with CSV-row line number (header is row 1) so the analyst review queue can show exactly which row failed. papaparse@^5.5 + @types/papaparse added. 16 new tests covering single + multi-row happy paths, header variants (4 forms), missing-column rejection, per-row errors (entry/date/duty/ior), multi-error reporting alongside valid rows, empty input, header-only input, Excel-stripped leading-zero filer codes. 701/701 pass; lint + typecheck clean; build green (24 routes — pure module).
